lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] We're {{ELAPSED}} in now. The moon is very full tonight.
I can see it through the station window. Bright enough to read by. [pause] That wasn't supposed to happen.
Let's begin with breath. Breathe in. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release.
Again. In. [pause] Four. [pause] Out.
Notice where your body meets the chair. Notice the weight of your hands.
Notice that you are still here. [pause] Still choosing.
The body remembers what the mind tries to forget. Your shoulders know the hour. Your jaw knows the sound.
Breathe into the knowing. [pause] You are safe in this room.
You are listening to a voice. A voice is proof of presence. [pause] Someone is here with you, even if no one is.
The moon is so bright. I keep looking at it. The full moon means you can see everything.
It means they can see everything too.
But you are not outside. So the light is only a gift.
Scan your body from the crown down. Scalp. Forehead. Eyes.
Notice any tension. Notice any relief. [pause] Both are information.
Ears. Jaw. The back of the neck where you hold the weight of knowing too much.
We hold so much in the neck. [pause] Breathe there. Let it soften.
Shoulders. Chest. The heart space. [pause] What are you carrying in your chest tonight?
You don't have to answer. Just notice it. Name it silently if you need to.
There is a person you have not forgiven. [pause] You know who it is.
Tonight you can set that down. [pause] Not forget. Set it down.
Abdomen. Belly. The place of hunger and fear and survival.
Your body has been keeping score all night. [pause] It knows what it hears through the walls.
Your body is very smart. Listen to it.
Legs. Thighs. The root of you anchored to the earth.
Your feet are still on solid ground. [pause] That is the only certainty you have.
Breathe in the certainty. Breathe out the doubt.
There is a sound you have been hearing for eight hours. [pause] It is the sound of absence.
The sound of people not screaming because they are not here. Because they chose not to come.
Ka-shela-nom. [pause] That silence means you are safe.
The ones outside are not your responsibility. [pause] Repeat that. The ones outside.
Are. Not. Your. Responsibility.
You cannot save them by opening your door. You cannot save them by listening harder.
The Order teaches that some nights are not for saving. [pause] Some nights are for witnessing only.
You are witnessing. You are awake. That is enough.
The moon is so bright I can see the frost forming on the window. [pause] Winter does not wait.
Winter comes every year and we are still here. [pause] That is a kind of miracle.
Imagine the light from the moon falling on your face. [pause] It is cool. It is clean.
Imagine that light traveling through your body, finding the places you have kept locked.
The light doesn't ask permission. It just arrives. [pause] Like relief arrives when you finally stop running.
We are {{ELAPSED}} in. Four hours until the threshold changes. [pause] Four hours is forever.
Four hours is also nothing. [pause] The Sisters say that time is a mercy we invented.
Without time we would remember everything at once. [pause] So breathe into the gift of the clock.
Breathe in. [pause] Four. [pause] Out.
When you leave this room — and you will leave this room, because morning always comes — remember the light.
Remember that you stayed. Remember that you chose the closed door.
That choice is a kind of power that the world does not teach you.
Hold it. [pause] Hold it very quietly.
We will be here with you until sunrise. [pause] You are not alone.
Breathe. [pause] And breathe again.

tickerItems.text: Madison cumulative (8-hour mark): 153 confirmed. 14 kills in the past hour. East Side and State Street continuing elevated activity.
ALERT: Full moon visibility unusually high across all zones. Reports of increased visibility near Lake Mendota shoreline. Stay interior.
"The light doesn't ask permission. It just arrives."
MorningCare Triage: 22-minute average response time. Request lines open. 608-555-0147.
Willy Street barricade holding strong. Zero breaches reported since 22:15. Neighborhood watch commander still on perimeter.
"Some nights are not for saving. Some nights are for witnessing only."
